title: Convert to smaller units (oz and lb)
description: Practice converting a US customary measure of mass to a smaller unit.
topic_path: Convert to smaller units (oz and lb)

hints: We need to convert $\blueD5$ pounds $\maroonD{4}$ ounces to ounces.

First, let's convert $\blueD5$ pounds to ounces.

$1 \text{ pound} = \greenD{16}\text{ ounces}$


$\blueD{5} \text{ pounds} = \blueD{5}\times \greenD{16}=\blueD{80}\text{ ounces}$

Now, we need to add $\maroonD{4}$ ounces.

$\blueD{80}\text{ ounces}+\maroonD{4}\text{ ounces}=84\text{ ounces}$

options.explanation:
Conversions| | | |||
- | | | |
$1$ mile $= 5{,}280$ feet| | $1$ minute $= 60$ seconds|| $1$ cup $= 8$ fluid ounces|
$1$ mile $= 1{,}760$ yards| |$1$ hour $= 60$ minutes|| $1$ pint $= 2$ cups|
$1$ yard $= 3$ feet||$1$ pound $= 16$ ounces ||$1$ quart $= 2$ pints|
$1$ foot $= 12$ inches||$1$ ton $= 2{,}000$ pounds || $1$ gallon $= 4$ quarts|

hints: We need to convert $\blueD{7}$ pounds $\maroonD{15}$ ounces to ounces.

First, let's convert $\blueD{7}$ pounds to ounces.

$1 \text{ pound} = \greenD{16}\text{ ounces}$


$\blueD{7} \text{ pounds} = \blueD{7}\times \greenD{16}=\blueD{112}\text{ ounces}$

Now, we need to add $\maroonD{15}$ ounces.

$\blueD{112}\text{ ounces}+\maroonD{15}\text{ ounces}=127\text{ ounces}$

hints: We need to convert $\blueD{10}$ pounds $\maroonD{10}$ ounces to ounces.

First, let's convert $\blueD{10}$ pounds to ounces.

$1 \text{ pound} = \greenD{16}\text{ ounces}$


$\blueD{10} \text{ pounds} = \blueD{10}\times \greenD{16}=\blueD{160}\text{ ounces}$

Now, we need to add $\maroonD{10}$ ounces.

$\blueD{160}\text{ ounces}+\maroonD{10}\text{ ounces}=170\text{ ounces}$
